import io
import string
import list

fn find_index(_ nums: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut i = 0
  mut result: Option<Int> = None
  while i < nums.len()
    match result
    when None
      match nums.get(i)
      when Some(n)
        if n == target
          result = Some(i)
        end
      when None
        # no-op
      end
    when Some(_)
      # already found; continue to drain the loop
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn main() -> Result<Unit, String>
  let line1 = io.read_line().ok_or("expected first line")?
  let tokens = string.split_whitespace(line1)

  mut nums: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  while i < tokens.len()
    match tokens.get(i)
    when Some(tok)
      match string.parse_int(tok)
      when Some(n)
        nums = list.push(nums, n)
      when None
        # skip non-integer tokens
      end
    when None
      # no-op
    end
    i = i + 1
  end

  let line2 = io.read_line().ok_or("expected second line")?
  let target = string.parse_int(string.trim(line2)).ok_or("invalid target")?

  match find_index(nums, target)
  when Some(idx)
    print("index=#{idx}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end

  Ok(())
end
